flashcards.back: Probability that a non-negative RV exceeds a threshold: P(X ≥ a) ≤ E[X]/a

flashcards.front: What information does Chebyshev's inequality use that Markov doesn't?
flashcards.back: Variance of the distribution (uses σ² rather than just mean)

flashcards.front: CLT requires what condition about sample size?
flashcards.back: Sample size n → ∞, though n ≥ 30 often used practically

flashcards.front: Which inequality is useful for bounded RVs?
flashcards.back: Markov (requires only non-negativity and finite mean)

flashcards.front: How does Chebyshev improve upon Markov's bound?
flashcards.back: Uses variance for tighter probability estimates around mean

flashcards.front: What distribution does CLT converge to?
Practical use of CLT in CS
flashcards.back: Approximate probabilities in randomized algorithms & statistical tests
When X is constant or has all mass at 0 and a value ≥ a
It's fundamental to probability theory, central to statistical inference
